lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: I do beseech you, sir, trouble yourself no further.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: O, pardon me: ’twill do me good to walk.

lines.speaker: LODOVICO
lines.text: Madam, good night; I humbly thank your ladyship.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Your honour is most welcome.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Will you walk, sir? O—Desdemona—

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: My lord?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Get you to bed on the instant; I will be returned forthwith:

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: dismiss your attendant there: look it be done.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: I will, my lord.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: How goes it now? he looks gentler than he did.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: He says he will return incontinent:

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: He hath commanded me to go to bed,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: And bade me to dismiss you.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Dismiss me!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: It was his bidding: therefore, good Emilia,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Give me my nightly wearing, and adieu:

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: We must not now displease him.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: I would you had never seen him!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: So would not I my love doth so approve him,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: That even his stubbornness, his cheques, his frowns—

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Prithee, unpin me—have grace and favour in them.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: I have laid those sheets you bade me on the bed.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: All’s one. Good faith, how foolish are our minds!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: If I do die before thee prithee, shroud me

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: In one of those same sheets.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Come, come you talk.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: My mother had a maid call’d Barbara:

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: She was in love, and he she loved proved mad

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: And did forsake her: she had a song of “willow;”

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: An old thing ’twas, but it express’d her fortune,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: And she died singing it: that song to-night

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Will not go from my mind; I have much to do,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: But to go hang my head all at one side,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: And sing it like poor Barbara. Prithee, dispatch.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Shall I go fetch your night-gown?

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: No, unpin me here.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: This Lodovico is a proper man.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: A very handsome man.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: He speaks well.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: I know a lady in Venice would have walked barefoot to Palestine

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: for a touch of his nether lip.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: The poor soul sat sighing by a sycamore tree, Sing all a

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: green willow: Her hand on her bosom, her head on her knee,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Sing willow, willow, willow: The fresh streams ran by her, and murmur’d

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: her moans; Sing willow, willow, willow; Her salt tears fell from her,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: and soften’d the stones; Lay by these:— Sing willow, willow, willow; Prithee,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: hie thee; he’ll come anon:— Sing all a green willow must be

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: my garland. Let nobody blame him; his scorn I approve— Nay, that’s

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: not next.—Hark! who is’t that knocks?

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: It’s the wind.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: I call’d my love false love; but what said he then?

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Sing willow, willow, willow:

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: If I court moe women, you’ll couch with moe men!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: So, get thee gone; good night. Mine eyes do itch;

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Doth that bode weeping?

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: ’Tis neither here nor there.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: I have heard it said so. O, these men, these men!

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Dost thou in conscience think—tell me, Emilia—

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: That there be women do abuse their husbands

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: In such gross kind?

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: There be some such, no question.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Wouldst thou do such a deed for all the world?

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Why, would not you?

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: No, by this heavenly light!

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Nor I neither by this heavenly light;

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: I might do’t as well i’ the dark.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Wouldst thou do such a deed for all the world?

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: The world’s a huge thing: it is a great price.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: For a small vice.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: In troth, I think thou wouldst not.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: In troth, I think I should; and undo’t when I had done.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Marry, I would not do such a thing for a joint-ring, nor

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: for measures of lawn, nor for gowns, petticoats, nor caps, nor any

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: petty exhibition; but for the whole world—why, who would not make her

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: husband a cuckold to make him a monarch? I should venture purgatory

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: for’t.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Beshrew me, if I would do such a wrong

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: For the whole world.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Why the wrong is but a wrong i’ the world: and having

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: the world for your labour, ’tis a wrong in your own world,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: and you might quickly make it right.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: I do not think there is any such woman.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Yes, a dozen; and as many to the vantage as would store the world they played for.

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: But I do think it is their husbands’ faults

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: If wives do fall: say that they slack their duties,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: And pour our treasures into foreign laps,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Or else break out in peevish jealousies,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Throwing restraint upon us; or say they strike us,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Or scant our former having in despite;

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Why, we have galls, and though we have some grace,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Yet have we some revenge. Let husbands know

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Their wives have sense like them: they see and smell

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: And have their palates both for sweet and sour,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: As husbands have. What is it that they do

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: When they change us for others? Is it sport?

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: I think it is: and doth affection breed it?

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: I think it doth: is’t frailty that thus errs?

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: It is so too: and have not we affections,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Desires for sport, and frailty, as men have?

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: Then let them use us well: else let them know,

lines.speaker: EMILIA
lines.text: The ills we do, their ills instruct us so.

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Good night, good night: heaven me such uses send,

lines.speaker: DESDEMONA
lines.text: Not to pick bad from bad, but by bad mend!

stage_directions.text: Enter Othello, Lodovico, Desdemona, Emilia and Attendants.

glosses.definition: emblem of forsaken love; the willow branch was worn as a sign of grief

glosses.phrase: the poor soul sat sighing by a sycamore tree
glosses.definition: opening line of a traditional ballad of abandoned love, sung by Desdemona on the night of her death
a cheap ring with parts that could be separated
Desdemona's final words to her maid on this earth

annotations.title: The Willow Song
annotations.body:
Desdemona's song — 'The poor soul sat sighing by a sycamore tree, sing willow, willow, willow' — is a real ballad, documented in Elizabethan and earlier sources under the title 'Willow, Willow'. The willow was the emblem of forsaken love; lovers wore willow garlands as visible signs of grief. Desdemona remembers the song from her mother's maid 'Barbary' — a detail with layered resonance: 'Barbary' names the North African coast, returning Othello's origins to the bedroom through song.

Shakespeare gives the moment a lullaby's stillness. The song reduces her tragedy to a folk object — something her body learned once and returns to without thought. This is the technical achievement: the scene's emotional devastation is expressed not through new language but through a song older than any of the characters, sung by someone about to die.
sources: Peter Burke, *Popular Culture in Early Modern Europe* (1978)

annotations.title: Emilia's catechism on women
annotations.body:
Emilia's argument — that women err because men betray, that a wife's wrongs are husbands' teachings — is the most substantial piece of proto-feminist reasoning in Shakespeare. The speech is structural, not digressive: it prepares the ground for Emilia's defiance in 5.2, where she will speak over her husband's prohibition and be killed for it.

Shakespeare does not position Emilia's reasoning as self-exculpation. Desdemona refuses the argument. But Emilia's catalogue — slack husbands, confiscated allowances, bodily wrongs — is delivered with the authority of experience, and Shakespeare lets it stand. The scene's last lines ('Let husbands know / Their wives have sense like them') are the theatrical grounds for what Emilia will later do.
sources: Carol Thomas Neely, *Broken Nuptials in Shakespeare's Plays* (1985)

trials.prompt: Desdemona sings the Willow Song she learned from her mother's maid 'Barbary'. Why is the name 'Barbary' significant in this scene?
options: It is a random folk name with no meaning
It names the North African coast — quietly bringing Othello's origins into the bedroom through song
It is a pseudonym Desdemona invents on the spot
It was the name of a famous Venetian composer

trials.prompt: Emilia's argument that women err because men betray them, and that 'their wives have sense like them', is:
options: A minor digression Shakespeare immediately abandons
A structural preparation for Emilia's defiance of Iago in 5.2, where she will speak over his prohibition and be killed for it
Borrowed directly from a Venetian law treatise
Meant ironically — Shakespeare is satirizing early feminism

trials.prompt: Desdemona asks Emilia whether there are women who would be unfaithful 'for all the world.' What does Desdemona's disbelief reveal?
options: That she has personal experience of infidelity she is not admitting
That her moral imagination remains intact even as her husband's unravels — she cannot conceive what Iago has instilled in Othello
That she is considering an affair herself
That she is testing Emilia's loyalty
